sections.paragraph:
λῃστής, οῦ, ὁ [ληίζομαι ‘plunder’, cp. λεία ‘booty’]

—1. ‘one who engages in forceful and illicit seizure of property’, robber, bandit Mt 21:13; 27:38; Mk 11:17; Lk 10:30; J 10:1, 8; 2 Cor 11:26. From this sense derives

—2. ‘one who engages in violent activity against established social order’, revolutionary, insurrectionist J 18:40 (cp. Mk 15:7).
